articles.title: Ukraine hits major oil terminal in Russia's St Petersburg
articles.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says the target is key infrastructure "that generates revenue for Russia's war".

articles.title: Unpatched Flaws Disclosed in Filesystem Bundled Into Millions of Embedded Devices
articles.summary: Security firm runZero has disclosed seven vulnerabilities in FatFs, a small filesystem library that lets a device read and write the FAT and exFAT formats used on USB drives and SD cards. The flaws matter because FatFs is nearly everywhere. It ships inside the firmware that runs security cameras, drones, industrial controllers, hardware crypto wallets, and other devices built on

articles.title: New Avalon Malware Framework Packs CrownX Ransomware Capabilities
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented modular malware framework codenamed Avalon that's distributed by means of a multi-stage phishing chain capable of bypassing traditional security controls. Avalon combines credential collection, lateral movement, remote access, recovery disruption, and ransomware execution, bringing together diverse functions under one

articles.title: Armored Likho Targets Government Agencies, Power Sector with BusySnake Stealer
articles.summary: A previously undocumented threat actor known as Armored Likho has been attributed to cyber attacks targeting government agencies and the electric power sector across Russia, Brazil, and Kazakhstan. "Armored Likho blends financially motivated campaigns targeting private individuals with targeted cyber espionage aimed at organizations," Kaspersky said in a technical analysis published today. "

articles.title: Ukrainian suspect hunted by police after Monaco bomb attack was 'disguised as a man'
articles.summary: Officials believe the suspect may not have acted alone and spent days casing out the scene of the attack.

articles.title: Polish PM warns critical months ahead in face of Russian threat
articles.summary: Tusk says Poland is preparing for "various" scenarios after media reports of a planned Russian attack.

articles.title: 'Hotter and hotter and hotter' - Europe's new climate in seven charts
articles.summary: Temperature records were smashed in June - and scientists warn this is a sign of things to come.

articles.title: Sudan’s people subject to ‘relentless’ drone strikes in El Obeid
articles.summary: The war in Sudan threatens to spiral further as the strategically-important town of El Obeid continues to face “relentless” drone attacks by advancing paramilitary militias, the UN’s human rights chief, Volker Türk, said on Friday.

articles.title: Ukraine: Rescuers work to save trapped residents after wave of Russian attacks
articles.summary: Aid agencies in Ukraine on Thursday rushed to respond to a massive uptick in deadly strikes by Russia, including one of the largest attacks on Kyiv to date.

articles.title: European Parliament Member Investigating Spyware Was Hacked With Pegasus
articles.summary: A new report from the Citizen Lab has revealed that former Member of the European Parliament Stelios Kouloglou had his mobile device repeatedly hacked with the notorious Pegasus spyware while serving on a committee that was tasked with investigating the abuse of such commercial surveillance tools in the bloc. "Through forensic analysis of his device, we found that the attackers could have had

articles.title: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
articles.summary: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.

articles.title: England's warmest June on record following historic heatwave
articles.summary: England saw its warmest June since records began in 1865 and the second warmest for the whole of the UK according to data just released by the Met Office.

articles.title: Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Iranian attack in Bahrain
articles.summary: The 15-member Security Council is meeting in emergency session on Thursday morning to address the escalating tensions in the Gulf region following Iran attacks in recent days on Bahrain and Kuwait while Tehran and Washington continue to trade strikes over the Strait of Hormuz chokepoint. Stay tuned for live updates.

articles.title: New "Bad Epoll" Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Unprivileged Users Gain Root, Hits Android
articles.summary: A newly disclosed Linux kernel flaw called Bad Epoll (CVE-2026-46242) lets an ordinary user with no special access take full control of a machine as root. It affects Linux desktops, servers, and Android, and a fix is out. Bad Epoll sits in the same small stretch of kernel code where Anthropic's most powerful AI model, Mythos, recently found a different bug. The AI caught one flaw and missed

articles.title: DeBriefed 3 July 2026: US faces scorching Independence Day | Record ocean temperatures | Vietnam’s EV surge
articles.summary: Welcome to Carbon Brief’s DeBriefed. An essential guide to the week’s key developments relating to climate... The post DeBriefed 3 July 2026: US faces scorching Independence Day | Record ocean temperatures | Vietnam’s EV surge appeared first on Carbon Brief .

articles.title: Brutal heat cancels Fourth of July events, from DC to Philadelphia
articles.summary: More than 165 million people are enduring dangerous heat and humidity, with temperatures expected to stay at 38C (100F) for several days.

articles.title: Pope urges Europe to do more for migrants while visiting gateway island
articles.summary: The pontiff pays tribute to migrants who lost their lives at sea at a cemetery on the Italian island of Lampedusa.

articles.title: Turbines turning from wind to sustainable products
articles.summary: Thousands of tonnes of non-recyclable wind turbine blades are coming to the end of their lives.

articles.title: UK temperatures forecast to exceed 30C as another heatwave imminent
articles.summary: Another heatwave is on its way and set to last significantly longer as Stav Danaos explains.

articles.title: Record-breaking temps in US as 4th July holiday begins
articles.summary: Record-breaking heatwave hits US as Fourth of July holiday weekend gets underway.

articles.title: NASA’s Hubble Captures Crimson Cloud Sparkling with White, Blue Stars
articles.summary: Blue and white stars shine brightly against crimson gas in this image from NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope.

articles.title: NASA Seeks Volunteers for New Yearlong Simulated Moon, Mars Mission
articles.summary: NASA is recruiting research participants for the agency’s next simulated deep space mission. Beginning no earlier than August 2027, research volunteers will spend one year living and working in interplanetary environments at the agency’s Johnson Space Center in Houston, operating under isolated conditions expected during crewed missions to the Moon or Red Planet. Insights from this […]

articles.title: LINK Spacecraft Set for Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift Observatory
articles.summary: A first-of-its-kind mission to raise the orbit of NASA’s Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory is poised for launch no earlier than Thursday, July 2, 5:09 a.m. EDT (9:09 p.m. UTC+12), from Kwajalein Atoll, part of the Republic of the Marshall Islands in the South Pacific Ocean. A robotic servicing spacecraft called LINK, built by Katalyst Space, will blast […]

articles.title: A device that revives eyeballs from dead donors could make eye transplants possible
articles.summary: It’s not easy to transplant a whole human eye. The surgery is difficult. And the eyes themselves start to degenerate as soon as they’ve left the body. When surgeons attempted it a few years ago, the newly-transplanted eye wasn’t able to see. But researchers believe they might have a solution: a device that maintains and…

articles.title: North Korean Hackers Publish 108 Malicious Packages and Extensions in PolinRider Campaign
articles.summary: The North Korean threat actors linked to the Contagious Interview campaign have been observed publishing 108 unique packages and web browser extensions spanning npm, Packagist, Go, and Google Chrome as part of an ongoing activity referred to as PolinRider. "The campaign remains active, and new malicious packages are likely to continue appearing as threat actors compromise maintainer accounts,

articles.title: PamStealer Uses Fake Maccy Sites and PAM Checks to Steal Mac Login Passwords
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new macOS information stealer called PamStealer that employs a series of clever tricks to infect systems and siphon sensitive data. The stealer, discovered by Jamf Threat Labs, is distributed as a compiled AppleScript (.scpt) file impersonating Maccy, a legitimate open-source clipboard manager. It has been codenamed PamStealer owing to its ability to

articles.title: Ransomware Groups Turn to Citrix Bleed 2, BYOVD, and Supply Chain Credentials
articles.summary: Threat actors associated with the Anubis ransomware operation have been observed exploiting the Citrix Bleed 2 (CVE-2025-5777) vulnerability to obtain initial access. "Although tactics differ between affiliates, common patterns emerged in tradecraft through use of legitimate Remote Management and Monitoring (RMM) tooling, credential access, and hands-on-keyboard procedures used for lateral

articles.title: As food shocks spread, citizens are showing more leadership than governments
articles.summary: People support efforts to protect forests and curb industrial food production, the results of the latest Global Citizens' Assembly show - but politicians have been slow to act The post As food shocks spread, citizens are showing more leadership than governments appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: With extreme heat now a public health crisis, local data can save lives
articles.summary: The impacts of extreme heat vary widely across countries and cities, and approaches tailored using data can target solutions at vulnerable groups The post With extreme heat now a public health crisis, local data can save lives appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: Ocean summit stays silent on new wave of offshore oil and gas expansion
articles.summary: At this month’s Our Ocean Conference in Mombasa, campaigners warned that ocean protection requires governments to halt new offshore fossil fuel projects The post Ocean summit stays silent on new wave of offshore oil and gas expansion appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: Cropped 1 July 2026: Heatwave scorches Europe | UK 2050 farm plan | What’s next for the High Seas Treaty
articles.summary: We handpick and explain the most important stories at the intersection of climate, land, food... The post Cropped 1 July 2026: Heatwave scorches Europe | UK 2050 farm plan | What’s next for the High Seas Treaty appeared first on Carbon Brief .

articles.title: 'Flamingo Revolution' takes off as thousands demand Albanian PM's resignation
articles.summary: Flamingos have become a national symbol of protest, triggered by opposition to a luxury development.

articles.title: Anguished families left to identify Venezuela quake victims at makeshift morgue
articles.summary: The disaster has overwhelmed local services, with bodies put outside or in tents for identification.

articles.title: France records 2,025 excess deaths at peak of heatwave as Europe braces for more extreme weather
articles.summary: Forecasters are warning of further extreme temperatures on the continent in the next few days.

articles.title: Row after Indian state drops eggs from school lunch menu
articles.summary: West Bengal's move to replace eggs in some school meals has sparked a wider debate over nutrition and choice.

articles.title: Scientist dubbed The Bogfather is restoring peatland to fight climate change
articles.summary: A childhood fascination with bogs led one Welsh scientist to try and help save the planet.

articles.title: Nasa launches mission to save falling space telescope
articles.summary: A Nasa-funded robot has blasted off to catch a falling telescope in mid-orbit and blast it back to safety before it burns up.

articles.title: Civilian dangers multiply as drones transform Ukraine's battlefield
articles.summary: As drones reshape the battlefield in Ukraine, they are also creating new and increasingly complex dangers for civilians, threatening recovery efforts, agriculture and global food security long after the fighting ends.

articles.title: Taking the world’s temperature: WHO chief spotlights global health emergencies
articles.summary: The hantavirus global outbreak is officially over, Ebola outbreaks are growing in Africa and responders are helping recovery efforts in quake-hit Venezuela, the World Health Organization (WHO) chief said on Thursday, announcing updates on global health emergencies.

articles.title: World News in Brief: Risk of atrocities in Sudan, UN programme prevents climate displacement, more civilians killed in Ukraine
articles.summary: Drone attacks by advancing rebel fighters who are besieging El Obeid, Sudan, continue to result in civilian casualties, putting around half a million civilians at grave risk of large-scale atrocities.

articles.title: Expanding areas under Israeli control in Gaza increase risks to civilians, UN warns
articles.summary: The continued expansion of areas under Israeli control in Gaza since October’s ceasefire agreement is placing civilians at greater risk and severely constraining humanitarian operations, the UN and humanitarian partners warned on Wednesday.

articles.title: NASA’s Hubble Spies Stellar Sparkler for July 4th
articles.summary: Red, white, and blue stars glitter like a sparkler being waved on a dark night in this new image from NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope.

articles.title: NASA’s Artemis II Breaks Agency Streaming Record
articles.summary: NASA’s live coverage of the Artemis II mission mission drew unprecedented public interest – including more than 149.4 million views of the launch, lunar flyby, splashdown on NASA-owned platforms, including the 24/7 streams covering the mission and the Orion spacecraft views – demonstrating strong, sustained global engagement throughout the mission. Around the Clock Live Broadcast NASA’s Artemis II Crew Launches to the Moon broadcast set unprecedented viewership records across the agency’s […]

articles.title: Why California’s carbon manure math doesn’t add up
articles.summary: Something stinks in California’s climate policies. Years ago, the state set up a system that pays cattle farmers across the country to turn the methane emitted from cattle manure into natural gas, encouraging the dairy sector to produce a gas we burn instead of one that just pollutes the air. It’s become wildly popular because…

articles.title: North Korea-Linked npm Packages Mimic Rollup Polyfills to Steal Developer Secrets
articles.summary: Threat actors with ties to North Korea have been linked to a fresh set of malicious npm packages that masquerade as Rollup polyfill tooling to facilitate remote access and data theft. According to JFrog, the packages "rollup-packages-polyfill-core" and "rollup-runtime-polyfill-core" mimic the legitimate "rollup-plugin-polyfill-node" project, down to the description, repository metadata, and
